start.speaker: SYSTEM
start.text: [i]The war room aboard the Lumen Thief. Harsh overhead lighting cuts sharp shadows across the central holo-table. Star charts and facility schematics hover in pale blue wireframe. Coffee dregs and stimulant wrappers litter every surface. Nobody has slept. Nobody pretends they will.[/i]

elia_calls_order.speaker: Elia
elia_calls_order.text: [i]Rapping her knuckles on the table.[/i] Everyone in. Seal the door. This is the last time we go over this—so listen like your life depends on it, because it does.

crew_assembles.speaker: SYSTEM
crew_assembles.text: [i]They file in. Rho leans against the bulkhead, arms folded across his chest plate. Vesper takes the chair furthest from the door—old sniper habit, eyes on every exit. Nyx perches on the edge of the table itself, staff across her knees. Jalen slides into his tech station, fingers already dancing across three screens. Elisira stands at parade rest beside the holo-table, datapad in hand.[/i]

elisira_intel_intro.speaker: Elisira
elisira_intel_intro.text: [i]Activating the holo-table. A facility schematic blooms into view—layered, detailed, annotated in her precise handwriting.[/i] I pulled this from a dead-drop my contact embedded in the Lattice's procurement chain. It cost them their cover. Let's not waste it.

facility_overview.speaker: SYSTEM
facility_overview.text: [i]The hologram rotates. A fortified compound—part research lab, part military installation. Three concentric rings of defense. Underground levels descending into bedrock. Ward signatures pulse in angry red along every chokepoint.[/i]

elisira_layout_detail.speaker: Elisira
elisira_layout_detail.text: Designation: Site Onyx. Three perimeter rings. Outer ring is automated—turrets, sensor nets, drone patrols on six-minute cycles. Middle ring is personnel—guards rotating in four-hour shifts. Inner ring...

elisira_layout_inner.speaker: Elisira
elisira_layout_inner.text: [i]Zooming in. The innermost structure pulses with ward-light.[/i] Inner ring is where it gets ugly. Lattice-woven barriers. Threshold locks that require living ward-signatures to pass. And the command center sits three levels below ground.

bee_tactical_overlay.speaker: BEE
bee_tactical_overlay.text: {BEE:: FACILITY_ANALYSIS: Site Onyx defensive topology mapped | STATUS: 94.2% schema confidence | DETAIL: subsurface resonance patterns suggest additional ward layers not visible on procurement schematics—recommend caution at depth}

nyx_ward_warning.speaker: Nyx
nyx_ward_warning.text: [i]Leaning forward, staff humming faintly.[/i] Those Lattice barriers aren't standard containment wards. I've seen this signature pattern before—they're layered. Break one, two more activate underneath. Like a hydra made of bad math.

nyx_ward_detail.speaker: Nyx
nyx_ward_detail.text: And the threshold locks? They'll burn out anyone who tries to brute-force them. I'm talking neural scorching. Permanent damage. We need finesse, not firepower, for those.

text_variants.nyx_trust_high: I've been studying the resonance patterns BEE flagged. I think I can unravel them—but I'll need time and no one shooting at me while I work.
text_variants.nyx_trust_low: I can probably crack them. Probably. No guarantees when the Lattice is involved.

choices.text: How long to dismantle each ward layer?
What happens if you can't crack them?
We'll make sure you have the time. What do you need?

nyx_time_estimate.speaker: Nyx
nyx_time_estimate.text: Three minutes per layer if I'm undisturbed. Six if someone's shooting. Infinite if I'm dead. So—priorities.

nyx_contingency.speaker: Nyx
nyx_contingency.text: [i]A flicker of something dark behind her eyes.[/i] Then we find another way in. Or we don't get in at all. The Lattice doesn't leave back doors—they leave traps that look like back doors.

nyx_requirements.speaker: Nyx
nyx_requirements.text: [i]Surprised—then a careful nod.[/i] Silence. Proximity to the ward anchor points. And someone watching my back who won't panic when the light show starts.

vesper_strategy_intro.speaker: Vesper
vesper_strategy_intro.text: [i]Standing, moving to the holo-table. She traces a route along the facility's eastern approach.[/i] Here's how I see it. Two teams. One loud, one quiet. The loud team hits the outer ring hard—draws every guard, every drone, every automated response to the eastern perimeter.

vesper_strategy_detail.speaker: Vesper
vesper_strategy_detail.text: Meanwhile, the quiet team slips in through the maintenance conduits on the western subsurface level. Jalen's scans show a thirty-second gap in the sensor net during shift rotation. That's our window.

rho_disagrees.speaker: Rho
rho_disagrees.text: [i]Pushing off the bulkhead, jaw set.[/i] Thirty seconds. You want to thread a needle in thirty seconds while people are shooting at us on the other side of the building. That's not a plan—that's a prayer.

rho_counter_proposal.speaker: Rho
rho_counter_proposal.text: I say we hit them with everything. Blow the eastern wall, pour through before they can regroup. Speed and violence. By the time they figure out what's happening, we're already inside.

text_variants.rho_trust_high: I've run breaches like this before. On Kael-7. We lost zero—because we didn't give them time to think.
text_variants.rho_trust_low: I know what I'm talking about. You want surgical? Fine. But don't come crying when surgical gets people killed.

vesper_rho_tension.speaker: Vesper
vesper_rho_tension.text: [i]Cold and precise.[/i] Speed and violence gets people killed when the building is laced with wards that trigger on kinetic impact. You blow that wall, Nyx's hydra wakes up, and we're fighting the building and the garrison.

rho_fires_back.speaker: Rho
rho_fires_back.text: [i]Voice rising.[/i] And your thirty-second window gets one team killed if the timing's off by three seconds. At least my plan keeps us together—

elia_takes_control.speaker: Elia
elia_takes_control.text: [i]Looking between them.[/i] You're both right. And you're both wrong. A full assault triggers the wards. A pure stealth approach leaves no fallback if something goes sideways. So we do both—but we do it smart.

player_tactics_check.speaker: SYSTEM
player_tactics_check.text: [i]Elia looks to you. The crew follows her gaze. This is where your input matters.[/i]

choices.text: [Tactics] Propose a coordinated timing strategy—assault draws response exactly when infiltration needs the gap widened.
Vesper's right—stealth first, force only as backup.
Rho has a point—overwhelming force means fewer variables.

tactics_success.speaker: SYSTEM
tactics_success.text: [i]You sketch a timing overlay on the holo-table. Assault hits at T-zero. Infiltration moves at T-plus-fifteen, when the garrison has fully committed east. A rolling distraction that builds, giving the quiet team progressively more room.[/i]

crew_impressed.speaker: Elisira
crew_impressed.text: [i]Studying the overlay. A single nod.[/i] Staggered escalation. The garrison commits deeper with each wave, pulling further from the western approach. It's clean.

elia_approves_tactics.speaker: Elia
elia_approves_tactics.text: [i]A ghost of a smile.[/i] That's the plan, then. Vesper, Rho—you both get your wish. Just on a timer.

tactics_failure.speaker: SYSTEM
tactics_failure.text: [i]Your timing proposal has gaps—Elisira spots the overlap where both teams would be exposed simultaneously. The crew exchanges glances.[/i]

elisira_corrects.speaker: Elisira
elisira_corrects.text: [i]Gently adjusting your overlay.[/i] The concept is sound, but the timing needs refinement. Here—if we offset the second wave by ninety seconds, the gap closes.

elia_approves_corrected.speaker: Elia
elia_approves_corrected.text: Good bones. Elisira's adjustment seals it. We run with this.

side_with_vesper.speaker: Vesper
side_with_vesper.text: [i]A measured nod.[/i] Stealth gets us to the objective without waking the whole compound. The assault team holds ready as contingency—overwhelming force in reserve, not as an opening move.

rho_reluctant_agree_vesper.speaker: Rho
rho_reluctant_agree_vesper.text: [i]Jaw working. Then a sharp exhale.[/i] Fine. But the second something goes wrong, I'm coming through that wall whether there are wards or not.

side_with_rho.speaker: Rho
side_with_rho.text: [i]A sharp, approving nod.[/i] Hit them hard enough that they're too busy surviving to notice anyone slipping through the cracks. Classic hammer-and-scalpel.

vesper_reluctant_agree_rho.speaker: Vesper
vesper_reluctant_agree_rho.text: [i]Lips thin. But she adjusts.[/i] Then we build in more redundancy for the infiltration route. If the assault is the primary action, the quiet team needs three exit paths, not one.

jalen_tech_options.speaker: Jalen
jalen_tech_options.text: [i]Spinning a display toward the group. Three options shimmer in green wireframe.[/i] Tech support. I've been working on some things. Pick your poison.

jalen_options_detail.speaker: Jalen
jalen_options_detail.text: Option one: I loop their sensor net. Gives infiltration team ghost status for about eight minutes before they detect the spoof. Option two: I crash their comms—garrison can't coordinate, but they'll know something's wrong immediately. Option three: I piggyback on their drone network and feed false positional data.

choices.text: [Tech] Can you run the sensor loop AND the drone piggyback simultaneously?
Sensor loop. Ghost the infiltration team.
Crash their comms. Blind and deaf is better than invisible.
Drone piggyback. Make them chase ghosts.

jalen_dual_tech_success.speaker: Jalen
jalen_dual_tech_success.text: [i]Eyes lighting up.[/i] You know what—yeah. If I reroute the buffer through BEE's processing matrix, I can stagger them. Sensor loop first, drone piggyback kicks in when the loop degrades. Seamless handoff.

bee_confirms_dual.speaker: BEE
bee_confirms_dual.text: {BEE:: PROCESSING_ALLOCATION: approved | STATUS: matrix capacity sufficient for dual-channel operation | DETAIL: estimated coverage extension from 8 minutes to 14.7 minutes with cascaded handoff protocol}

jalen_dual_tech_fail.speaker: Jalen
jalen_dual_tech_fail.text: [i]Frowning, running numbers.[/i] I... wish. But the bandwidth isn't there. Running both would create interference patterns they'd detect faster than running neither. Pick one, and I'll make it count.

choices.text: Sensor loop. Ghost the infiltration team.
Drone piggyback. Make them chase phantoms.

jalen_sensor_loop.speaker: Jalen
jalen_sensor_loop.text: Sensor loop it is. Eight minutes of invisibility. After that, you're on your own eyes and instincts. Use every second.

jalen_comms_crash.speaker: Jalen
jalen_comms_crash.text: Comms crash. When it hits, they'll have about forty-five seconds of total chaos. No orders, no coordination, no calling reinforcements. Make those forty-five seconds ugly.

jalen_drone_spoof.speaker: Jalen
jalen_drone_spoof.text: Drone spoof, locked. Their own surveillance will tell them we're hitting from the north while we're already inside from the west. Beautiful misdirection.

team_composition_intro.speaker: Elia
team_composition_intro.text: [i]Pulling up the crew roster on the holo-table. Seven names. Two columns.[/i] Two teams. Infiltration gets in quiet, reaches the command center, pulls the data. Assault holds the perimeter, draws fire, and covers extraction. Who goes where matters.

elisira_strengths_briefing.speaker: Elisira
elisira_strengths_briefing.text: For infiltration, you want precision shooters, ward-breakers, and someone who can navigate the subsurface layout. For assault, you want firepower, durability, and the ability to hold a position under sustained pressure.

character_strengths_overview.speaker: SYSTEM
character_strengths_overview.text: [i]The holo-table lights each crew member's profile as Elisira speaks. Combat ratings, specializations, equipment loadouts—all rendered in cold blue light.[/i]

elisira_assessments.speaker: Elisira
elisira_assessments.text: Rho—heavy weapons, breaching, sustained fire. Born for assault. Vesper—precision marksmanship, overwatch, counter-sniper. Valuable either side, but her range makes her devastating on assault perimeter. Nyx—ward disruption is non-negotiable for infiltration. She goes quiet side.

elisira_assessments_2.speaker: Elisira
elisira_assessments_2.text: Jalen—tech support operates remotely, but physical placement matters for signal strength. Closer to the facility core means stronger spoofing. Elia—close-quarters specialist. Either role, but her blade work shines in corridors. And myself—I can lead either team.

elisira_assessments_avyanna.speaker: Elisira
elisira_assessments_avyanna.text: [i]Looking at you.[/i] Avyanna—your shard resonance gives you ward sensitivity. You'll feel Lattice traps before anyone else. That makes you an asset on infiltration. But the call is yours.

player_insight_check.speaker: SYSTEM
player_insight_check.text: [i]You study the facility layout, the crew profiles, the ward signatures. Something about the defensive pattern nags at you.[/i]

choices.text: [Insight] Study the ward pattern more closely—something doesn't add up.
Stick with Elisira's assessment. She knows what she's doing.

insight_success.speaker: Avyanna
insight_success.text: [i]Tracing the ward pattern on the holo-table.[/i] Wait. These ward signatures—they're not just defensive. They're channeling something. The inner ring isn't protecting the command center. It's feeding it. If the assault team hits the outer wards hard enough, it might actually strengthen the inner barriers.

nyx_confirms_insight.speaker: Nyx
nyx_confirms_insight.text: [i]Eyes widening.[/i] She's right. Sympathetic resonance. The outer wards are tethered to the inner ones. Kinetic disruption on the perimeter would cascade inward as reinforcement. The assault team needs to suppress, not destroy—or I'll be cracking twice the barriers underground.

elia_adjusts_plan.speaker: Elia
elia_adjusts_plan.text: [i]Nodding slowly.[/i] Good catch. Rho—you heard her. Assault team suppresses, doesn't shatter. Controlled violence. Can you do that?

rho_controlled_violence.speaker: Rho
rho_controlled_violence.text: [i]A beat. Then a grudging nod.[/i] Controlled violence. I can do controlled. Might not enjoy it as much.

insight_failure.speaker: SYSTEM
insight_failure.text: [i]The ward patterns blur together. You sense something beneath the surface, but it slips away before you can articulate it. The feeling of almost-knowing lingers like a word on the tip of your tongue.[/i]

team_assignment_choice.speaker: Elia
team_assignment_choice.text: [i]Arms crossed.[/i] I have a recommended split. But you've earned a voice in this, Avyanna. How do you want to run it?

choices.text: Infiltration: Nyx, Elisira, Jalen, and me. Assault: Elia, Rho, Vesper. Close-quarters dominance on the perimeter.
Infiltration: Nyx, Elisira, Rho, and me. Assault: Elia, Vesper, Jalen, Avyanna coordinating. Mixed-range flexibility.
Infiltration: Nyx, Elisira, Jalen, Rho. Assault: Elia, Vesper, and me. Tactical precision underground, I hold the line above.
Go with Elia's recommendation.

assault_cq_reaction.speaker: Rho
assault_cq_reaction.text: Perimeter assault with Elia and Vesper. Close, mid, and long range covered. [i]Cracking his knuckles.[/i] They won't get past us.

vesper_cq_react.speaker: Vesper
vesper_cq_react.text: Overwatch on the assault. I'll keep their heads down while you two close the distance. Clean geometry.

text_variants.vesper_trust_high: I'll keep every exit covered. Nobody flanks us. Nobody.
text_variants.vesper_trust_low: Standard overwatch. Don't get in my sight lines.

assault_mixed_reaction.speaker: Elia
assault_mixed_reaction.text: Mixed composition gives us flexibility. Vesper on overwatch, me on close-quarters anchor, Jalen managing comms from the assault position. Avyanna coordinates between teams.

jalen_mixed_react.speaker: Jalen
jalen_mixed_react.text: [i]Nodding, already recalculating signal routes.[/i] If I'm topside with the assault team, my spoofing range increases by forty percent. Closer to the facility's broadcast array means better signal injection.

assault_precision_reaction.speaker: Elisira
assault_precision_reaction.text: Surgical team below. Nyx for wards, Jalen for tech, Rho for breaching—and I lead. Above ground, Elia and Vesper hold with Avyanna.

elia_precision_react.speaker: Elia
elia_precision_react.text: [i]A slow nod.[/i] You want to be on the line. I respect that. Vesper and I will make sure nobody comes through that perimeter.

elia_default_split.speaker: Elia
elia_default_split.text: My recommendation: Nyx, Elisira, and Jalen on infiltration with Avyanna. Myself, Rho, and Vesper on assault. It plays to everyone's strengths.

gear_check_intro.speaker: Elia
gear_check_intro.text: Gear check. Every piece of equipment, every charge, every med-kit—verified and verified again. Assault team, what's your loadout?

gear_assault_check.speaker: SYSTEM
gear_assault_check.text: [i]Equipment spreads across the table. Ammunition counts, explosive charges, suppression grenades, emergency beacons. The smell of gun oil and ozone fills the room.[/i]

rho_gear.speaker: Rho
rho_gear.text: Rotary cannon—full drum, two spares. Six shaped charges, four concussion grenades. Armor's been patched and sealed. [i]Patting his chest plate.[/i] She'll hold.

vesper_gear.speaker: Vesper
vesper_gear.text: Clarity and Witness—both zeroed at range. Two hundred rounds standard, forty armor-piercing. Ghillie mesh for the overwatch position. Emergency sidearm.

text_variants.vesper_trust_high: [i]Quieter.[/i] And a second medkit. In case someone else needs it more than I do.

elia_gear.speaker: Elia
elia_gear.text: [i]Drawing one blade, inspecting the edge.[/i] Twin blades. Sharpened this morning. Four flash charges for corridor work. Light armor—I need mobility more than plating.

nyx_gear.speaker: Nyx
nyx_gear.text: Threshold staff—fully charged. Ward disruption kit: three resonance dampeners, two null-anchors, one emergency collapse sigil. [i]She hesitates.[/i] And the shard fragment, in case I need to improvise.

elisira_gear.speaker: Elisira
elisira_gear.text: Dual pistols, suppressed. Sixty rounds subsonic, twenty standard. Lockpick set—both mechanical and electronic. Facility map loaded to three separate devices in case we lose one.

jalen_gear.speaker: Jalen
jalen_gear.text: Mobile tech rig. Signal jammer, spoofing suite, three bypass modules. [i]Holding up a small device.[/i] And this—a localized EMP. One shot. Kills everything electronic in a twenty-meter radius, including my own gear. Absolute last resort.

bee_gear_analysis.speaker: BEE
bee_gear_analysis.text: {BEE:: LOADOUT_ASSESSMENT: all teams within operational parameters | STATUS: ammunition reserves at 94% optimal | DETAIL: recommend infiltration team carry additional null-anchors—subsurface ward density exceeds initial projections by factor of 1.4}

nyx_extra_anchors.speaker: Nyx
nyx_extra_anchors.text: [i]Pulling two more null-anchors from the supply locker.[/i] BEE's right. Better to have them and not need them. [i]Tucking them into her belt.[/i] That's five total. Should be enough. Has to be enough.

personal_moments_intro.speaker: SYSTEM
personal_moments_intro.text: [i]The briefing winds down. Equipment checked, plans locked, contingencies mapped. A strange quiet settles—the kind that lives between the last order and the first step into danger. People drift into pairs. Small conversations. The words that matter.[/i]

vesper_rho_moment.speaker: SYSTEM
vesper_rho_moment.text: [i]Vesper and Rho stand apart by the weapons rack. They don't look at each other. They don't need to.[/i]

rho_to_vesper.speaker: Rho
rho_to_vesper.text: [i]Low, only for her.[/i] If the line breaks—

rho_vesper_silence.speaker: SYSTEM
rho_vesper_silence.text: [i]A pause. Rho nods. Something passes between them that words would only diminish.[/i]

jalen_nyx_moment.speaker: Jalen
jalen_nyx_moment.text: [i]Sliding a small device across the table to Nyx.[/i] Here. Emergency comms relay. If everything else goes dark, this runs on ward energy. Your staff should keep it powered.

nyx_accepts_device.speaker: Nyx
nyx_accepts_device.text: [i]Turning it over in her fingers.[/i] You built this? When?

jalen_shrugs.speaker: Jalen
jalen_shrugs.text: [i]Shrugging.[/i] Couldn't sleep. Figured one of us should make something useful out of insomnia.

elisira_elia_moment.speaker: SYSTEM
elisira_elia_moment.text: [i]Elisira and Elia stand over the holo-table, the facility map still rotating between them. Their reflections ghost across the blue light.[/i]

elisira_to_elia.speaker: Elisira
elisira_to_elia.text: [i]Quietly.[/i] This is the one, isn't it. The mission we don't come back from unchanged.

elia_to_elisira.speaker: Elia
elia_to_elisira.text: [i]A breath.[/i] We come back. All of us. That's the only version of this I'm willing to plan for.

player_personal_moment.speaker: SYSTEM
player_personal_moment.text: [i]The quiet stretches. Someone is going to find you before you find them.[/i]

choices.text: Find a quiet corner alone. Collect your thoughts.
Check on the crew. Make sure everyone's okay.
Study the facility map one more time.

avyanna_alone.speaker: SYSTEM
avyanna_alone.text: [i]You find a corner near the viewport. Stars drift past—indifferent to what's about to happen. Your shard pulses once against your chest. Warm. Almost like a heartbeat.[/i]

avyanna_checks_crew.speaker: SYSTEM
avyanna_checks_crew.text: [i]You make the rounds. A nod to Rho. A touch on Jalen's shoulder. A look that says everything to Nyx. The crew notices. They always notice.[/i]

avyanna_studies_map.speaker: SYSTEM
avyanna_studies_map.text: [i]The facility turns slowly in blue light. You memorize corridors, chokepoints, ward junctions. If something goes wrong, you want to know every path by heart.[/i]

elia_finds_you.speaker: Elia
elia_finds_you.text: [i]Appearing beside you. She moves like smoke when she wants to.[/i] You ready?

text_variants.elia_trust_high: [i]Her hand finds your shoulder. Brief. Warm. The grip of someone who means it.[/i] Whatever happens in there—you've already proven you belong on this crew. Don't forget that.
text_variants.elia_trust_low: [i]Arms folded. Professional distance. But she came to find you, and that says something.[/i] Stay sharp. Follow the plan. We'll get through this.

choices.text: Ready. Floor holds.
I'm terrified. But I trust this crew.
Let's bring them hell.

elia_honesty_response.speaker: Elia
elia_honesty_response.text: [i]Something softens in her expression. Just for a moment.[/i] Good. Terrified means you understand the stakes. And trust... trust is the only thing that's going to get us through those walls.

elia_final_check.speaker: Elia
elia_final_check.text: Final positions. Assault team, sound off.

assault_sound_off_start.speaker: SYSTEM
assault_sound_off_start.text: [i]The assault team responds based on earlier choices. Each member acknowledges their role with practiced brevity.[/i]

assault_cq_sound_off.speaker: Rho
assault_cq_sound_off.text: Breach and suppress. Rotary cannon loaded, charges prepped. Ready.

assault_cq_elia.speaker: Elia
assault_cq_elia.text: Corridor clearing. Close-quarters dominance. Blades sharp. Ready.

assault_cq_elisira.speaker: Elisira
assault_cq_elisira.text: Precision eliminations. Dual pistols, suppressed. Ready.

assault_cq_nyx.speaker: Nyx
assault_cq_nyx.text: Ward disruption. Threshold staff active. Ready to light them up.

assault_mixed_sound_off.speaker: Elia
assault_mixed_sound_off.text: Close-quarters anchor. I'll clear the path. Ready.

assault_mixed_vesper.speaker: Vesper
assault_mixed_vesper.text: Precision fire. Counter-sniper. Clarity and Witness loaded. Ready.

assault_mixed_nyx.speaker: Nyx
assault_mixed_nyx.text: Ward disruption and locality adjustments. Ready to bend their doors.

assault_mixed_avyanna.speaker: Avyanna
assault_mixed_avyanna.text: [i]Voice steadier than she feels.[/i] Lattice tracking. I'll map their wards. Ready.

assault_precision_sound_off.speaker: Elisira
assault_precision_sound_off.text: Surgical strike lead. Dual pistols. Precision only. Ready.

assault_precision_rho.speaker: Rho
assault_precision_rho.text: Clean breaches. Shaped charges, minimal collateral. Ready.

assault_precision_nyx.speaker: Nyx
assault_precision_nyx.text: Ward disruption without signature flare. Stealth variant protocols. Ready.

assault_precision_jalen.speaker: Jalen
assault_precision_jalen.text: Real-time route optimization. Mapping exits as we go. Ready.

hold_sound_off_start.speaker: SYSTEM
hold_sound_off_start.text: [i]The hold team responds. Positions locked. Roles clear.[/i]

hold_cq_jalen.speaker: Jalen
hold_cq_jalen.text: Extraction pilot. Ship prepped, engines hot. Ready to fly the exit.

hold_cq_avyanna.speaker: Avyanna
hold_cq_avyanna.text: [i]Hands steady on her comms tablet.[/i] Coordination. Hostile tracking. Comms relay. Ready.

hold_mixed_rho.speaker: Rho
hold_mixed_rho.text: Long-range breach support. Shaped charges on standby. Ready.

hold_mixed_jalen.speaker: Jalen
hold_mixed_jalen.text: Timing coordination. Extraction ready. Ready to pull you out.

hold_precision_vesper.speaker: Vesper
hold_precision_vesper.text: Long-range dominance. No one gets close. Ready.

hold_precision_avyanna.speaker: Avyanna
hold_precision_avyanna.text: [i]Gripping her tablet.[/i] Coordination. Hostile tracking. I'll keep you all connected. Ready.

final_acknowledgments.speaker: Elia
final_acknowledgments.text: [i]Looking at each person.[/i] Roles are clear. Timing is tight. If anything goes wrong, we adapt—but we don't leave anyone behind.

waffle_briefing_status.speaker: Waffle.bat
waffle_briefing_status.text: [i]{WAFFLE.BAT// TACTICAL_SUMMARY: all teams assigned | COMMS_STATUS: encrypted channels active | NOTE: I will be monitoring all channels. You are not alone in there.}[/i]

bubbles_briefing_prep.speaker: Bubbles
bubbles_briefing_prep.text: [i]Through speakers:[/i] Medical kits staged at both insertion points. Emergency extraction protocols loaded. Be safe out there.

cinnamon_briefing_ready.speaker: Cinnamon
cinnamon_briefing_ready.text: Ship systems locked for combat ops. Drill bay sealed. All non-essential subsystems powered down. Ready.

vesper_addition.speaker: Vesper
vesper_addition.text: Extraction window is six hours. We hit hard, we hit fast, we get out. No heroics. No unnecessary risks.

rho_grin.speaker: Rho
rho_grin.text: [i]Grinning, but his eyes are serious.[/i] And if they try to stop us, we remind them why the Lumen Thief has a reputation.

crew_floor_holds_response.speaker: SYSTEM
crew_floor_holds_response.text: [i]The response is immediate. Overlapping. Muscle memory. No one asked permission. No one gave orders. The pattern just happened.[/i]

moment_of_silence.speaker: SYSTEM
moment_of_silence.text: [i]Silence. Not fear. Solidarity. Every person here chose this. Every person here knows what's at stake.[/i]

elia_move_out.speaker: Elia
elia_move_out.text: [i]Strapping her blades.[/i] Move out in ten. Let's bring them hell.

end_scene.speaker: SYSTEM
end_scene.text: [i]The crew disperses to final preparations. The weight of the moment hangs in the air. This is it. The climax. The fight that changes everything.[/i]

choices.text: [Continue]
